description: Target table for Phase VI reference campaign. All targets carry explicit distributionModel. Targets are labeled by evidence tier: toy-placeholder (arbitrary synthetic), derived-synthetic (analytically constructed from known model), or evidence-grade (externally motivated). Phase VI rule D-P6-005: every target must have an explicit distributionModel.

targets.label: bosonic-mode-1-ratio
targets.notes: Ratio of second bosonic eigenvalue to first; 40% uncertainty. Gaussian: no physical justification for heavy tails at this tier.

targets.label: bosonic-mode-2-ratio
targets.notes: Ratio of third bosonic eigenvalue to first; 40% uncertainty. Gaussian: symmetric placeholder.

targets.label: bosonic-mode-3-ratio
targets.notes: Ratio of fourth bosonic eigenvalue to first; 40% uncertainty. Gaussian: symmetric placeholder.

targets.label: fermionic-mode-1-ratio
targets.notes: Ratio of second fermionic eigenvalue to first; 40% uncertainty. Gaussian: symmetric placeholder.

targets.label: fermionic-mode-2-ratio
targets.notes: Ratio of third fermionic eigenvalue to first; 40% uncertainty. Gaussian: symmetric placeholder.

targets.label: bosonic-mode-1-ratio-derived-synthetic
targets.notes: Derived-synthetic: ratio analytically constructed from a known SU(2) toy model spectrum. Gaussian is correct — analytically derived ratios have symmetric error propagation; gaussian-asymmetric would require physical justification for directional uncertainty (physicist guidance).

targets.label: fermionic-mode-1-ratio-small-sample
targets.notes: Small-sample example: N=5 samples, nu=N-1=4 (physicist guidance: nu=4 gives scale factor sqrt(4/2)=sqrt(2)~1.414, meaningfully wider than Gaussian; never use nu<=2 as variance is undefined). Not evidence-grade.
